summary: This is a brief teaser post promoting Joel's third strategy letter about barriers to switching. Joel argues that understanding barriers of entry is critical when trying to win customers from competitors, and that most people underestimate how significant these barriers are. He warns that failing to address switching costs will result in business failure.
key_points: Understanding barriers of entry is essential when competing for users of rival products
Most people underestimate how difficult it is to get users to switch products
Failure to address switching barriers leads to business failure
money_quotes: When you're trying to get people to switch from a competitor to your product, you need to understand barriers of entry, and you need to understand them a lot better than you think, or people won't switch and you'll be waiting tables.
key_insight: Most companies fatally underestimate the switching costs that prevent users from adopting their product over a competitor's.
